_about: Gateway Prometheus metrics (internal/gateway/metrics.go) whose NAME can be over-read. The disciplined tree discloses each divergence in the HELP text; this file pins those fences so a future metric cannot drop one silently.

rows.invited_intent: a cache hit rate - reads served from a cache divided by lookups
rows.literal: the fraction of kernel submissions served by the vDSO fast path (an adjudication-bypass rate), not cache reads per lookup

rows.invited_intent: cache hits - how often a trust/result cache was effective
rows.literal: kernel submissions served by the vDSO fast path (a count of adjudication bypasses), not cache hits
rows.disclosure: served by the vDSO fast path

rows.invited_intent: maximum request latency, or a hung-request detector
rows.literal: the age of the oldest in-flight request AT SCRAPE TIME; a request that hangs and finishes between scrapes is never seen
gaps: The HELP discloses 'currently in flight' but not the scrape-time blindness; a stronger fence would name it.

rows.invited_intent: cumulative or aggregate memory demand / memory pressure
rows.literal: the most-recent single served request's memory plan - a last-request gauge, not an aggregate counter
rows.disclosure: last-request gauge, not a cumulative counter

rows.invited_intent: the distribution of real adjudication decisions the kernel made
rows.literal: decisions by winning rung INCLUDING rung=vdso, which means no adjudication happened - a bypass row sits among the real decisions
rows.disclosure: passive; re-derived off the hot path
